page: Amazon Web Services x2gd.4xlarge is a memory-optimized server powered by custom 64-bit ARMv8 AWS Graviton2 processors utilizing the Neoverse-N1 microarchitecture. Operating at a clock speed of 2.5 GHz, it features 16 dedicated physical cores with a 1:1 thread-to-core ratio, which eliminates simultaneous multithreading overhead. The instance is equipped with 256.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high resource density of 16.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. Storage is supported by 950 GB of local NVMe SSD instance store volumes, and network connectivity is provisioned at a baseline speed of 5 Gbps, all managed via the AWS Nitro hypervisor.

Benchmark results indicate average performance across most compute and memory operations. The stress-ng multi-core CPU test scored 24289.25, while the Geekbench multi-core score reached 8543.0. Memory bandwidth tests showed 947.22 GB/sec for cached small-block reads and 155.82 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads. However, single-threaded performance is a notable tradeoff, with Geekbench single-core (962.0) and single-threaded gzip compression (20.32 GB/sec) falling into the weaker performance tiers. Passmark memory score also registered in the lower tier at 1761.22.

Given the high memory-to-vCPU ratio and integrated NVMe SSD storage, this server is designed for memory-intensive workloads that benefit from local scratch space. It demonstrates average performance in database operations with a score of 10266.45 on Passmark and Redis SET operations reaching 18.95 million operations per second. The ARM64 architecture provides qualitative cost efficiency by offering high memory density at a lower resource-to-cost ratio compared to traditional x86 memory-optimized instances. It is suitable for in-memory databases, caching layers, and memory-bound data processing tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services x2gd.4xlarge is an ARM64 memory-optimized server powered by a 16-core AWS Graviton2 processor running at 2.5 GHz. It features 256.0 GB of RAM, providing a high 16.0 GB per vCPU ratio, and includes 950 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, it delivers 5 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmarks show average multi-core performance, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 8543.0 and Redis SET operations reaching 18.95 million operations per second. However, single-threaded capabilities are weaker, as evidenced by a Geekbench single-core score of 962.0. Lacking AVX instruction sets, this instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-bound applications. It is best suited for in-memory databases, caching layers, and data processing workloads requiring high memory density and local scratch space.
og_description: Amazon Web Services x2gd.4xlarge is an ARM64 memory-optimized server featuring 16 dedicated Graviton2 cores, 256 GB of RAM, and 950 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, designed for memory-intensive database workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services x2gd.4xlarge is an ARM64 server with 16 Graviton2 cores, 256 GB RAM, and 950 GB NVMe SSD storage for memory-intensive workloads.
tagline: An ARM64 memory-optimized instance featuring high RAM density and local NVMe storage for memory-intensive databases and caching workloads.
bullet_points: 16 dedicated ARMv8 Graviton2 cores running at 2.5 GHz
256 GB of system memory providing 16 GB per vCPU
950 GB of integrated local NVMe SSD instance storage
5 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on the Nitro hypervisor
Average multi-core performance with weaker single-threaded execution
Optimized for in-memory databases, caching layers, and data processing
